rule_in_enclosed_space.short: IN — enclosed spaces, rooms, buildings, containers
rule_in_enclosed_space.example: in the room, in a box, in the car, in a hotel

rule_in_area.short: IN — countries, cities, regions, large open areas
rule_in_area.example: in France, in London, in the north, in the park

rule_on_surface.short: ON — surfaces (horizontal or vertical), things seen as flat
rule_on_surface.example: on the table, on the floor, on the wall, on the ceiling

rule_on_transport.short: ON — public transport and large vehicles
rule_on_transport.example: on the bus, on a train, on a plane, on a ship

rule_at_specific_point.short: AT — specific points, addresses, precise locations
rule_at_specific_point.example: at the station, at home, at the top, at the door

rule_at_event.example: at the party, at school, at work, at a concert

rule_in_long_period.short: IN — months, years, centuries, seasons, parts of the day
rule_in_long_period.example: in January, in 2023, in the morning, in summer

rule_on_day.short: ON — days of the week, specific dates, named days
rule_on_day.example: on Monday, on the 5th, on my birthday, on New Year's Day

rule_at_time.short: AT — clock times and fixed time points
rule_at_time.example: at 5pm, at noon, at midnight, at dawn

rule_by_deadline.short: BY — no later than a certain point (deadline)
rule_by_deadline.example: by Friday, by noon, by the end of the week

rule_until_continuous.short: UNTIL/TILL — continuously up to a point in time
rule_until_continuous.example: until midnight, till Monday, until she arrives

rule_for_duration.short: FOR — over a period of time (how long)
rule_for_duration.example: for two hours, for years, for a long time, for a week

rule_to_destination.example: go to school, travel to France, drive to work

rule_for_purpose.short: FOR — purpose, benefit, or intended recipient
rule_for_purpose.example: for you, work for a company, for fun, a gift for her

rule_with_accompaniment.short: WITH — accompaniment, using a tool or ingredient
rule_with_accompaniment.example: with friends, with a knife, pay with cash, coffee with milk

rule_from_origin.short: FROM — starting point, origin, or source
rule_from_origin.example: from London, from the beginning, a letter from her

rule_of_possession.short: OF — belonging, content, or part of a whole
rule_of_possession.example: the top of the hill, a cup of tea, a friend of mine

rule_about_topic.short: ABOUT — concerning a topic or subject
rule_about_topic.example: talk about work, worried about money, a book about history

rule_irregular_collocation.short: Fixed phrase — this preposition does not follow spatial logic. It must be memorized.
rule_irregular_collocation.example: good AT, interested IN, depend ON, responsible FOR

clusters.title: IN / ON / AT — Place
clusters.subtitle: Where exactly are you?
clusters.contrast_note: IN = inside an area or space. ON = touching a surface. AT = a precise point. Context decides.

clusters.title: IN / ON / AT — Time
clusters.subtitle: When does it happen?
clusters.contrast_note: IN = long periods. ON = days and dates. AT = clock times and fixed moments.

She was born ___ a cold December morning.
We usually go skiing ___ winter.
The store opens ___ 8 in the morning.
He started his new job ___ Monday.
They got married ___ June 1998.
I always feel tired ___ the afternoon.
The ceremony will be held ___ the 15th of August.
Everything goes quiet ___ midnight.
She called me ___ my birthday.
She usually calls her parents ___ Sunday evenings.

He wrote his first novel ___ his twenties.

There were no computers ___ the nineteenth century.
The library opens ___ 9 am and closes at 8 pm.

I prefer working ___ the morning when everything is quiet.

clusters.title: BY / UNTIL / FOR — Time Spans
clusters.subtitle: How long, and when does it end?
clusters.contrast_note: BY = deadline (finished no later than). UNTIL = continuous up to a point. FOR = total duration.

cards.sentence: Please submit your report ___ Friday.
She studied ___ three hours without a break.
We waited at the station ___ the last train arrived.
I need this done ___ noon at the latest.
They have been friends ___ over twenty years.
He kept working ___ he couldn't keep his eyes open.
Can you finish the painting ___ the end of the month?
The museum stays open ___ 9 o'clock in the evening.
The builders worked ___ six months to complete the extension.
You must return the library book ___ next Thursday.
They danced ___ the early hours of the morning.
She has been living abroad ___ the past five years.
He needs to call the client ___ 3 pm.
She kept all of his letters ___ the day she died.
They were stuck in traffic ___ two hours.
Can you wait ___ I get back?
He saved up ___ months before he could buy the car.
The proposal must be submitted ___ end of business today.
He stayed ___ everyone else had left the party.
She has been working at the same company ___ thirty years.
We need to leave ___ midday to catch the ferry.
The shop is open ___ 10 pm every day.
She slept ___ fourteen hours after the surgery.
I'll have the report ready ___ the morning.
He held onto the rope ___ his arms gave out.
She worked overtime ___ weeks to finish the project.
Could you hold the line ___ she is available?
The application deadline is ___ the 30th of June.
They waited ___ the storm passed before setting off.
She's been on the phone ___ an hour.
All invoices must be paid ___ the end of the quarter.
He practised guitar every day ___ he could play the full song.
I'll keep the table reserved ___ 8 pm, but after that it's gone.
The food bank has been running ___ over a decade.
Make sure the contract is signed ___ close of play Friday.
She played the piano ___ she forgot all about the time.
He waited ___ the very last moment to buy his train ticket.
He had been on the waiting list ___ almost two years.
The band played non-stop ___ midnight.
She needs to complete the online course ___ the end of the month or lose access.

clusters.title: TO / FOR / WITH
clusters.subtitle: Direction, purpose, and company
clusters.contrast_note: TO = movement or direction. FOR = purpose or recipient. WITH = accompanying or using.

cards.sentence: She walked ___ the library after school.
He bought flowers ___ his mother.
She cut the bread ___ a sharp knife.
They are travelling ___ Rome next week.
This sandwich is ___ you — I made it specially.
He came home ___ two bags of groceries.
She sent a long email ___ her manager.
He goes jogging ___ his dog every morning.
She mixed the flour ___ butter and sugar.
He drove her ___ the hospital immediately.
She brought a bottle of wine ___ the party.
They rented a cottage ___ a week by the sea.
He paid ___ the drinks in cash.
She signed the document ___ a pen she borrowed from the receptionist.
The coach spoke ___ the team before the match.
She is looking ___ a new flat closer to work.
He arrived at the event ___ his whole family in tow.
They donated the money ___ a local food bank.
She is saving up ___ a trip to South America.
He sent a postcard ___ his friends back home.
She cooked the pasta ___ olive oil and garlic.
We're going ___ the cinema tonight — do you want to come?
He did it ___ fun, not because anyone asked him to.
The room was decorated ___ balloons and streamers.
She applied ___ a scholarship to study overseas.
He flew ___ New York on business last spring.
She wrote the letter ___ a fountain pen.
She offered her seat ___ an elderly passenger.
They charged me extra ___ the checked baggage.
He sprinkled the salad ___ a little salt and pepper.
She ran ___ the door when the bell rang.
They gathered there ___ a specific purpose, though no one said what.
He filled the bath ___ warm water and a few drops of lavender oil.
She returned the book ___ the library on time.
They sell fresh bread here ___ only one pound a loaf.
He cleaned the windows ___ a damp cloth.
She handed the report ___ her supervisor before the deadline.
They are looking ___ someone to lead the project.
He finished the puzzle ___ help from his daughter.
She went ___ the doctor after three days of not feeling well.

clusters.title: FROM / OF / ABOUT
clusters.subtitle: Origins, content, and topics
clusters.contrast_note: FROM = starting point or source. OF = part, content, or belonging. ABOUT = the topic or subject.

cards.sentence: She received a letter ___ her old friend in Canada.
He told me everything ___ his trip to Morocco.
This is a picture ___ my grandmother as a young woman.
Where are you originally ___?
They argued ___ money for hours.
The roof ___ the old house was leaking badly.
She comes ___ a long line of musicians.
The documentary is ___ the lives of deep-sea fishermen.
She got a scholarship ___ the best university in the country.
The smell ___ fresh bread filled the whole street.
He complained ___ the noise coming from upstairs.
This tea comes all the way ___ a small region in Sri Lanka.
The bottom ___ the sea is full of life we haven't discovered yet.
She knows everything ___ the history of that region.
He recovered quickly ___ his illness.
The title ___ the film comes from a famous poem.
They talked ___ their plans for the summer for hours.
She learned to cook ___ her grandmother.
The price ___ the ticket includes all taxes and fees.
They were curious ___ the strange light in the sky.
He was exhausted ___ the long journey.
A large part ___ the city was damaged in the flood.
She knows a lot ___ traditional Japanese architecture.
The flavour ___ this dish comes from a blend of spices.
He has been living far ___ his family for many years.
She published an article ___ the effects of social media on teenagers.
The news ___ the discovery surprised the entire scientific community.
He translated the text directly ___ the original Latin.
She was reading a book ___ the life of Marie Curie.
The value ___ the painting has increased dramatically over the years.
The wool in this jumper comes ___ alpacas raised in Peru.
She's always talking ___ her plans to move abroad.
A third ___ the profits went to local charities.
He graduated ___ a top engineering school last year.
She's been writing a column ___ the challenges facing new parents.
The end ___ the story was completely unexpected.
She heard the news ___ a colleague she bumped into at the supermarket.
He's been warned ___ the risks several times.
The handle ___ the door was loose and kept turning without opening it.
She borrowed the recipe ___ an old book she found in the attic.

clusters.title: Fixed Phrases
clusters.subtitle: These don't follow rules — you just learn them.
clusters.contrast_note: These verb + preposition and adjective + preposition pairs are fixed. There is no spatial logic. Memorize them as units.

cards.sentence: He has always been very good ___ languages.
She is really interested ___ marine biology.
Everything depends ___ the weather tomorrow.
She feels very proud ___ her daughter's achievement.
He is responsible ___ the entire project.
They listened carefully ___ the instructions.
She agreed ___ everything he said.
He is married ___ a doctor he met in university.
She is afraid ___ flying, so she takes the train.
They applied ___ a grant to fund their research.
She is always tired ___ doing the same routine every day.
The bottle is full ___ sand from the beach.
This design is very similar ___ the one they used last year.
Her approach is quite different ___ what I expected.
She is grateful ___ all the support she received.
The region is famous ___ its olive oil and wine.
He was angry ___ his colleague for not telling him sooner.
She is worried ___ the results of the exam.
He finds it hard to concentrate ___ one thing for too long.
We can always rely ___ her to get the job done.

This ring belonged ___ my great-grandmother.

She dreams ___ owning her own restaurant one day.
She's been waiting ___ a reply for over two weeks.
He just sat there and stared ___ the screen without moving.
Did you remember to pay ___ the parking?

She has suffered ___ migraines since she was a teenager.
The argument resulted ___ both of them refusing to speak to each other.
She doesn't believe ___ making promises she can't keep.
He insisted ___ paying for everyone's dinner.
She eventually succeeded ___ convincing the board to approve the plan.
He has always specialised ___ tax law.
She is very keen ___ learning new skills.
He is not very good ___ remembering names.
She felt ashamed ___ what she had done.
He is very fond ___ classical music.
She is capable ___ much more than she lets on.
He is passionate ___ environmental issues.
She's been complaining ___ her back for months.

She is very sensitive ___ criticism.
He is aware ___ the risks involved.
They had to apologise ___ being late to the meeting.
She is addicted ___ coffee and can't function without it.
He takes great pride ___ his work.
She is allergic ___ cats, so she can't visit their house.
He gets on very well ___ his new colleagues.
He was disappointed ___ the outcome of the vote.
